At 0305 B/26, HMS Unruffled cleared her signal timed 0245 B/26 in which it was reported that 'operation Seaman' had been completed. The text of the signal was as follows; Operation completed. Expect to pass 40°30'N, at 0900 B/27 thence vicinity Brindisi. (5)

27 Aug 1943 HMS Unruffled (Lt. J.S. Stevens, DSO, DSC, RN) torpedoed and sank the Italian transport Città di Spezia (2474 GRT, built 1929) about 40 nautical miles, 103° of Brindisi, Italy in position 40°36'N, 18°37'E.

The Italian vessel was on a trip from Brindisi to Valona and was escorted only by a CANT Z.501 seaplane of 141^Squadriglia. She had a crew of forty-seven civilians and eleven naval personnel and was transporting 131 Military and forty-seven naval passengers. Thirty-nine were killed or missing. The seaplane attacked the submarine with two 100-kg A/S bombs then alighted to pick up five severely wounded. More aircraft, the torpedo boat Giuseppe Cesare Abba and the corvettes Scimittara and Pomona were sent to the scene to assist the rescue and hunt the submarine.
0935 B/27 - Sighted a Cant aircraft circling to the northward.

0955 B/27 - Sighted the masts of a merchant vessel. Commenced attack. The target was seen to be a medium sized merchant vessel of modern design. There was passenger accommodation amidships. No surface escort was seen.

1038 B/27 - In position 40°36'N, 18°37'E fired a salvo of four ships from 4300 yards. Three hits were observed. The vessel listed sharply to starboard and settled by the stern. Unruffled now went deep and retired from the area.
1850 B/27 - A convoy of small craft was seen making for Valona. Two schooners, two trawlers and a gunboat were seen. (5)
29 Aug 1943 At 0735 B/29, while patrolling off Brindisi, HMS Unruffled (Lt. J.S. Stevens, DSO, DSC, RN), sighted two northbound torpedo boats passing to seaward.
At 1030 B/29, another torpedo boat was seen proceeding northwards but this one was close inshore.
At 1555 B/29, two torpedo boats were seen proceeding southwards keeping close inshore. (5)
30 Aug 1943 Around 1700 B/30, HMS Unruffled (Lt. J.S. Stevens, DSO, DSC, RN), sighted two F-boats entering Brindisi harbour. (5)
31 Aug 1943 At 0835 B/31, while patrolling off Brindisi, HMS Unruffled (Lt. J.S. Stevens, DSO, DSC, RN), sighted a torpedo boat patrolling two miles to the east of Brindisi.
At 0900 B/31, another torpedo boat was seen to leave the harbour. Both torpedo boats then joined company and proceeded to the south.

At 1125 B/31, destroyers and / or torpedo boats were seen to leave Brindisi. They then patrolled in pairs to the east and north-east of the harbour about 2 miles out. They all returned to harbour around 1315 B/29.
At 1145 B/31, white smoke was seen above Brindisi. It was now considered that the enemy had left harbour due to an air attack.
At 2045 B/31, HMS Unruffled surfaced and set course to patrol off Valona.

2 Sep 1943 HMS Unruffled (Lt. J.S. Stevens, DSO, DSC, RN) left patrol to return to Malta in accordance with the Capt. S.10's signal timed 1102 B/29 which had contained the following orders; 1) Leave Brindisi area to pass through position 40°40'N, 18°52'E at 0200 B/1 thence to patrol off Valona. 2) Leave for Malta passing through position 40°15'N, 19°07'E at 0500 B/2, position 39°40'N, 19°03'E, position 38°46'N, 18°30'E at 0500 B/3, position 36°05'N, 16°50'E at 0500 B/4 and finally through position 35°45'N, 16°38'E to R/V with the minesweeper 5 miles east of Q.B.B. 147 (mined area) at 0800/5. You are to proceed on the surface on the 3rd. (45)
5 Sep 1943 HMS Unruffled (Lt. J.S. Stevens, DSO, DSC, RN) ended her 20th war patrol (19th in the Mediterranean) at Malta. [No time given in the patrol report.] (5)

26 Sep 1943 At 0930 A/26, HMS Unruffled (Lt. J.S. Stevens, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN) departed Malta for her 21st war patrol (20th in the Mediterranean). She was ordered to patrol off Corsica and the Gulf of Genoa.
Passage through the Sicilian war channel was made together with HMS Unshaken (Lt. J. Whitton, RN). They were escorted by HMS BYMS 2028 (Skr. J.R. Clark, RNR).
At 1830 B/27, in approximate position 37°22'N, 09°21'E, the two submarines and the escort all parted company with each other.

Further routing for HMS Unruffled was as follows; Diving by day through positions 38°05'N, 09°37'E at 2230 A/27, 39°01'N, 10°13'E at 1600 A/28 and 42°01'N, 10°13'E at 0500 A/30. The exact patrol zone would be signalled later.

29 Sep 1943 In a signal timed 1630 A/29, HMS Unruffled (Lt. J.S. Stevens, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN) and HMS Unshaken (Lt. J. Whitton, RN) were assigned the following patrol zones; Proceed to patrol in zones 'SQ' and 'SR' respectively both south of 43°00'N. (46)
30 Sep 1943 At 2100 A/30, HMS Unruffled (Lt. J.S. Stevens, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN) entered her patrol area, zone 'SQ'. Patrol was established in the northern approaches to Bastia. (5)

1 Oct 1943 At 0645 A/1, when a rain squall cleared, HMS Unruffled (Lt. J.S. Stevens, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN), which is patrolling the northern approaches to Bastia, sighted two vessels to the east of Bastia. One was identified as an UJ boat while the other was seen to be a small coaster of the engines aft type. This coaster had a heavy AA armament and a radar array on her masthead. It is thought this was a flak-ship. Both vessels were patrolling constantly altering course.
At 0700 A/1, six landing craft were seen proceeding to seaward.
At 0715 A/1, the UJ boat and flak-ship proceeded inshore. Visibility was variable throughout the day due to rain squalls. Landing craft of various types were seen to evacuate Bastia.
The UJ boat and flak-ship sighted were most likely the UJ 2209 which was escorting the transport KT 19 on a trip from Livorno to Bastia. They arrived at Bastia around 0930 B/1.

The following landing craft left or arrived at Bastia on 1 October 1943 [according to the KTB (log) of the Seetransportstelle (sea transport 'office') Bastia]; 0100 B/1 - F 522, F 621, F 613, F 181, F 503 and F 291 departed for Elba. 0300 B/1 - SF 109 and SF 125 departed for Elba. 0500 B/1 - F 609, F 617, F 622 and F 625 arrived. 0700 B/1 - F 609, F 617, F 622 and F 625 departed again after having loaded. F 249, F 518, F 619, F 423 and F 450 arrived as did F 456, F 554, F 610 and F 486. 0900 B/1 - F 249, F 518, F 619, F 423 and F 450 departed again after having loaded as did F 456, F 554, F 610 and F 486. 1800 B/1 - F 451, F 296, F 515 and F 611 arrived. F 515 was lightly damaged after the four F-boats had been attacked by US aircraft about 10 miles from Bastia around 1630 B/1. 2010 B/1 - F 451, F 296, F 515 and F 611 departed again after having loaded. F 360, F 514, F 777, F 422 and F 488 arrived. 2100 B/1 - F 514, F 777, F 422 and F 488 departed again after having loaded very quickly. 2400 B/1 - F 456, F 486, F 554, F 609 and F 610 arrived.
Meanwhile at 2200 B/1, the KT 19 had departed for Livorno escorted by UJ 2209.

The transport Nicoline Maersk (4194 GRT, built 1925) also departed for Livorno but there no mention of her in the KTB (log) of the 22nd UJ Flotilla but according to the KTB Seetransportstelle (sea transport 'office') Livorno they all arrived there around the same time.] It seems likely the Nicoline Maersk was escorted by the motor minesweeper R 161. (47)

2 Oct 1943 While patrolling in the northern approaches to Bastia, HMS Unruffled (Lt. J.S. Stevens, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN) reported even more landing craft and barges being used for the evacuation of Bastia.

The following landing craft left or arrived at Bastia on 2 October 1943 [according to the KTB (log) of the Seetransportstelle (sea transport 'office') Bastia]; 0100 B/2 - F 456, F 486, F 554, F 609 and F 610 departed having quickly loaded after having arrived only about an hour before. 0200 B/2 - F 503, F 518, F 249 and F 621 arrived. 0215 B/2 - F 205, F 515, F 622, F 424 and F 522 arrived. 0300 B/2 - F 503, F 518, F 249 and F 621 departed as did F 205, F 515, F 622, F 424 and F 522 shortly afterwards. 0700 B/2 - The German transports KT 8 (834 GRT, built 1943) and Glückauf (former Italian Carbonello A. 1593 GRT, built 1943) arrived from Livorno (departed around 2040 B/1). They were escorted by the motor minesweepers R 189, R 198 and R 199. [HMS Unruffled makes no mention of having sighted them.] 0730 B/2 - The self propelled barge Karl arrived. 0910 B/2 - F 488, F 360, F 422, F 513 and F 777 arrived. 0950 B/2 - F 488, F 360, F 422, F 513 and F 777 departed again, apparently having loaded very quickly. 1000 B/2 - F 451, F 296, F 611 and F 760 arrived. Also the German transport KT 31 (834 GRT, built 1943) arrived from Livorno. Apparently she was not escorted and also not sighted by HMS Unruffled. 1150 B/2 - F 451, F 296, F 611 and F 760 departed. 1200 B/2 - F 450, F 291, F 519 and F 423 arrived. 1300 B/2 - F 450, F 291, F 519 and F 423 departed again. 1315 B/2 - Nine L-boats (not identified) arrived. 1400 B/2 - The nine L-boats departed again while the Siebel Ferries SF 125, SF 109 and SF 231 arrived. 1500 B/2 - F 616, F 518, F 249, F 621, F 456, F 486, F 609 and F 610 arrived. 1550 B/2 - F 616, F 518, F 249, F 621, F 456, F 486, F 609 and F 610 departed again. 1700 B/2 - F 554, F 503, F 424, F 622, F 542, F 522 and F 181 arrived. 1815 B/2 - F 554, F 503, F 424, F 622, F 542, F 522 and F 181 departed again. 2200 B/2 - KT 31 departed. 2245 B/2 - F 596, F 795, F 625, F 183 arrived. 2315 B/2 - F 596, F 795, F 625, F 183 departed as did the self propelled barge Karl. 2400 B/2 - The transport Glückauf departed for Porto Ferraio under to of the tug Vittorio Veneto. They were escorted by the motor minesweepers R 189, R 198 and R 199. (48)
3 Oct 1943 HMS Unruffled (Lt.Cdr. J.S. Stevens, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN) sighted several more enemy landing craft during the night.

HMS Unruffled also attacked the German transport Sassari (former Italian Sassari, former French Sidi-Mabrouk, 2485 GRT, built 1917) with torpedoes north-east of Bastia, Corsica, France. The target was not hit. She was being escorted by the motor minesweepers R 212 and R 215. They were on passage from Livorno (departed around 2300 B/2) to Bastia.
Following the attack, Unruffled was hunted by R 212 but the submarine managed to escape.
0555 A/3 - Sighted a westbound merchant vessel to the southward. Closed to attack. The target was a three-island tramp of about 2000-3000 tons. She was in ballast.

0619 A/3 - In position 42°44'N, 09°32'E fired a salvo of four torpedoes from 4500 yards. No hits were obtained. A MAS boat was seen hunting in the area after the attack. She dropped eight depth charges but these were not close at all. [There is no mention in the German reports of one of the R-boats dropping depth charges.]
0820 A/3 - All was quiet. Commenced to close the shore to see where the merchant vessel had anchored.

0830 A/3 - Sighted a sloop type of vessel patrolling parallel to the coast. She was transmitting on Asdic and obviously aware of the presence of the submarine. Meanwhile several fires were seen in the town of Bastia which obviously was about to fall. The sloop sighted must have been the SG 11.

During the day, again many landing craft, motor boats and barges were carrying out the evacuation of the German Army. The sloop mentioned above remained patrolling off the shore all day to provide cover for these craft loading up.

1730 B/3 - Sighted masts and smoke inshore to the north-west. These proved to belong to a small engines aft type freighter of about 1000 tons. Lt. Stanley endeavoured to close but the final range was about 4000 yards and given the small size of the target torpedo fire was withheld as also her speed could not be properly estimated from that range.

2100 B/3 - Retired to the north to comply with the Capt. S.8's signal timed 1300 A/3 ordering Unruffled to patrol in zone 'SQ north' and 'SP' outside the 100 fathom line. As no traffic from Bastia was entering these area's Lt. Stanley decided to patrol to the north of Gorgona Island in the hope of intercepting traffic going to Livorno.

The following landing craft left or arrived at Bastia on 3 October 1943 [according to the KTB (log) of the Seetransportstelle (sea transport 'office') Bastia]. However due to fall of Bastia the report may be incomplete; 0030 B/3 - F 291, F 611, F 451, F 296 and F 356 arrived. 0300 B/3 - F 291, F 611, F 451, F 296 and F 356 departed again. Around the same time F 456, F 486, F 609, F 610 and SF 171 arrived. 0345 B/3 - F 456, F 486, F 609, F 610 and SF 171 departed again. All landing vessels which arrived after this time were withheld for the final phase of the evacuation but no record was kept on their identities. The last F-boat left at 2030 B/3. 0600 B/3 - The transports KT 19 (834 GRT, built 1943) and Giorgio (????) arrived coming from Livorno. 0646 B/3 - The transport Sasseri (see above) arrived from Livorno with her escorts, R 212 and R 215. Sasseri was sunk in an air attack later the same day. 1600 B/3 - The KT 19 and Giorgio departed for Livorno. (5)

4 Oct 1943 At 2030 A/4, HMS Unruffled (Lt.Cdr. J.S. Stevens, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN) set course to proceed to the northward to comply with the orders in the Capt. S.8's signal timed 1230 A/4 in which Unruffled was ordered to patrol in zone 'SO'. (5)
5 Oct 1943 HMS Unruffled (Lt.Cdr. J.S. Stevens, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN) attacked a merchant vessel in a convoy with four torpedoes to the south-west of Sestri Levante, Italy.

The convoy seen was made up of the German transports Gonneville (former French, 1724 GRT, built 1913), Nicoline Maersk (former Danish, 4194 GRT, built 1925), San Pedro (former French, 5947 GRT, built 1931), KT 8 (834 GRT, built 1943) and KT 14 (834 GRT, built 1943). The German tanker Pallas (former French), 5260 GRT, built 1925) had originally departed Livorno with them but quickly returned due to engine trouble.] The convoy was escorted by the UJ boat UJ 2209 and the motor minesweepers R 14, R 198 and R 199. The convoy was on passage from Livorno (departed around 1800 A/4-1900 A/4) to Genoa (arrived around 1130 B/5). Apparently the attack was not noticed by the Germans. [Note that the German time had changed from time zone 'B' to time zone 'A' on 4 October 1943.]
It looked like the small motor minesweepers were not sighted by HMS Unruffled and the two KT transports were mistaken for UJ boats.]

0729 A/5 - Sighted a northbound merchant vessel flying two barrage balloons coast crawling to the south-east. Closed to attack. The vessel was soon seen to be part of a convoy of three merchant vessels escorted by three UJ-boats. The target was a low built vessel of about 5000 tons with a vertical squat funnel and a cruiser stern. One small tramp was inshore of her and one vessel of medium size with no funnel was astern of her. The escorts were disposed one ahead and one on either beam of the rear ship.
0810 A/5 - In position 44°13'N, 09°18'E fired a full salvo of four torpedoes from a range of 3500 yards. No hits were heard. Also no counter attack developed.
0845 A/5 - Left patrol to proceed to Algiers. (49)

5 Oct 1943 In a signal timed 2330 A/5, HMS Unruffled (Lt. J.S. Stevens, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN) reported the following to the Capt. S.8; 1) Torpedoes expended regret all missed. 2) Returning through position 43°00'N, 09°01'E at 0215 A/6 thence via HMS Unseen's (Lt. M.L.C. Crawford, DSC and Bar, RN) patrol area as in your signal timed 1530 A/27 proceeding on the surface from 0600 A/8 to make rendezvous off Algiers 0630 A/9. (50)

6 Oct 1943 In a signal timed 0642 A/6, the Capt. S.8 sent the following orders to HMS Unruffled (Lt. J.S. Stevens, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN); After passing through position 43°00'N, 09°01'E at 0215 A/6 proceed diving by day through positions 42°01'N, 06°51'E at 0415 A7 and 40°02'N, 05°49'E at 0630 A/8. Thence on the surface at the Commanding Officers discretion through position 38°00'N, 03°48'E at 0130 A/9 to make rendezvous with the escort for Algiers in position 37°05'N, 03°19'E at 0900 A/9. (51)
9 Oct 1943 HMS Unruffled (Lt. J.S. Stevens, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN) ended her 21st war patrol (20th in the Mediterranean) at Algiers. [No time is given in the patrol report.] (5)
17 Oct 1943 At 1300 A/17, HMS Unruffled (Lt.Cdr. J.S. Stevens, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN) departed Algiers for Gibraltar. (5)

20 Oct 1943 In a signal timed 1559 A/20, the Flag Officer Gibraltar sent the following orders to HMS Unruffled (Lt. J.S. Stevens, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN); Do not cross Meridian 04°35'W before daylight/21. La Grandiere (?) and HMS Witherington (Lt.Cdr. R.B.S. Tennant, RN) and two merchant ships (Gouverneur General Lepine (French, 3509 GRT, built 1923) and Sidi-Brahim (British (former French, 2427 GRT, built 1910)) will sail at 0001 A/21 through position 36°26'N, 04°00'W for Malaga, speed 10 knots. Also 24 L.C.I's will be approaching Gibraltar from eastward at 0900 A/21. [Apparently these arrived at Gibraltar already on the 20th as none arrived there on the 21st.] (52)
21 Oct 1943 At 1330 A/21, HMS Unruffled (Lt.Cdr. J.S. Stevens, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN) arrived at Gibraltar from Algiers. (5)
4 Nov 1943 At 1145 A/4, HMS Unruffled (Lt.Cdr. J.S. Stevens, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN) departed Gibraltar for the U.K.

Due to the eastbound passage of German U-boats through the Straits of Gibraltar the sailing of HMS Unruffled, which had been scheduled for 26 October, had been postponed. In the meantime the submarine carried out A/S exercises of Gibraltar during four days.

She was routed through the following positions; 'Q' = 35.53'N, 06.15'W at 1720 A/4. 'R' = 36.37'N, 07.24'W at 0120 A/5. 'T' = 36.51'N, 09.13'W at 2200 A/5. 'S' = 42.01'N, 13.10'W at 0100 A/9. 'K' = 43.00'N, 13.22'W at 1400 A/9. Further routing would be signalled by the Flag Officer Submarines (F.O.S.).
[We have been unable to find a signal from the F.O.S. to Unrivalled but she was to follow route 'brown'.]
Later HMS Unruffled shifted to route 'red' as had been ordered in the F.O.S. signal timed 1214 A/11. (53)
15 Nov 1943 At 1730 A/15, HMS Unruffled (Lt.Cdr. J.S. Stevens, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN) arrived at Falmouth.
She was escorted there by the submarine chaser Chasseur 13 which had joined the submarine around 0745 A/15 in position 180° - Bishop Rock - 10 miles. (5)
16 Nov 1943 HMS Unruffled (Lt.Cdr. J.S. Stevens, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN) departed Falmouth for Portsmouth. She was escorted by the submarine chaser Chasseur 13.
They spent the night of 16/17 November at Dartmouth and the night of 17/18 November at Yarmouth. (5)
18 Nov 1943 HMS Unruffled (Lt.Cdr. J.S. Stevens, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN) arrived at Portsmouth. (5)
28 Nov 1943 HMS Unruffled (Lt.Cdr. J.S. Stevens, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN) departed Portsmouth for Sheerness. She made the passage in convoy CE 225 [No further details available for the moment]. (54)
29 Nov 1943 HMS Unruffled (Lt.Cdr. J.S. Stevens, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN) arrived at Sheerness from Portsmouth. (3)
30 Nov 1943 HMS Unruffled (Lt.Cdr. J.S. Stevens, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN) proceeded from Sheerness to Tilbury where she was to refit. (3)
3 Dec 1943 HMS Unruffled (Lt.Cdr. J.S. Stevens, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN) was taken in hand for refit at the Green and Silley Weir Ltd. Shipyard at Tilbury. (3)
7 May 1944 With her refit completed, HMS Unruffled (Lt. R.F. Park, RN), proceeded from Tilbury to Sheerness where D/G trials were carried out. (55)
8 May 1944 HMS Unruffled (Lt. R.F. Park, RN) proceeded from Sheerness to the Chatham Dockyard where torpedoes were to be embarked. (55)
9 May 1944 HMS Unruffled (Lt. R.F. Park, RN) proceeded from the Chatham Dockyard to Sheerness.
At 1830 B/9, she departed Sheerness for Portland. (55)
10 May 1944 Around 1545 B/10, HMS Unruffled (Lt. R.F. Park, RN) arrived at Portland from Sheerness. (55)
11 May 1944 Around 0640 B/11, HMS Unruffled (Lt. R.F. Park, RN) departed from Portland to Plymouth where she arrived around 1555 B/11. (55)
13 May 1944 HMS Unruffled (Lt. R.F. Park, RN) conducted exercises off Plymouth. (55)

1 Jun 1944 Around 0830 B/1, HMS Shakespeare (Lt. D. Swanston, DSC and Bar, RN) and HMS Unruffled (Lt. F. Park, RN) departed Plymouth for the Clyde. They were escorted by HMS White Bear (Cdr. C.W. Sabine, OBE, RN). (56)
3 Jun 1944 Around 1000 B/3, HMS Unruffled (Lt. R.F. Park, RN) arrived at Rothesay from Plymouth. She was now assigned to training duties while being attached to the 7th Submarine Flotilla. (57)
5 Jun 1944 HMS Unruffled (Lt. R.F. Park, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. (57)
6 Jun 1944 HMS Unruffled (Lt. R.F. Park, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. (57)
25 Jun 1944 HMS Unruffled (Lt. R.F. Park, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. (57)

25 Jun 1944 HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) proceeded from Rothesay to Campbeltown. En-route she ran speed trails on the Arran measured mile and acted as target for HMS Unruffled (Lt. R.F. Park, RN) which was conducting attack exercises. (58)

26 Jun 1944 HMS Una (Lt. A.G. Chandler, RNR) conducted gunnery exercises off Rothesay together with HMS Unseen (T/Lt. T.D. Wood, DSC, RNVR) and HMS Unruffled (Lt. R.F. Park, RN). The 'target' for these gunnery exercises was being towed by HMS Blade (T/A/Lt.Cdr. S.T. Wenlock, RNR). (59)

29 Jun 1944 At 1700 B/29, HMS Unruffled (Lt. R.F. Park, RN) and HMS Unseen (T/Lt. T.D. Wood, DSC, RNVR) departed Rothesay for St. John's, Newfoundland. They were escorted until 2340 B/30 by HMS Milford (Lt.Cdr. G.G. Slade, RN). (5)

9 Jul 1944 Around 0845 Z/9, HMS Unruffled (Lt. R.F. Park, RN) and HMS Unseen (T/Lt. T.D. Wood, DSC, RNVR) arrived at St. John's, Newfoundland from Rothesay. They were escorted in by HMCS Eyebright (T/A/Lt.Cdr. H.L. Quinn, RCNVR) which had joined around 1900 Z/8. (5)

11 Jul 1944 Around 1145 O/11, HMS Unruffled (Lt. R.F. Park, RN) (Lt. R.F. Park, RN) and HMS Unseen (T/Lt. T.D. Wood, DSC, RNVR) departed St.John's for Halifax. They were escorted by HMCS Eyebright (T/A/Lt.Cdr. H.L. Quinn, RCNVR). (60)

14 Jul 1944 Around 1100 P/14, HMS Unruffled (Lt. R.F. Park, RN), HMS Unseen (T/Lt. T.D. Wood, DSC, RNVR), and their escort, HMCS Eyebright (T/A/Lt.Cdr. H.L. Quinn, RCNVR), arrived at Halifax from St.John's. (61)
22 Jul 1944 Around 1130 B/22, HMS Unruffled (Lt. R.F. Park, RN) departed Halifax for Bermuda. She was escorted by HMCS St. Lambert (T/Lt. R.C. Hayden, RCNVR). (60)
26 Jul 1944 Around 1400 P/26, HMS Unruffled (Lt. R.F. Park, RN) and HMCS St. Lambert (T/Lt. R.C. Hayden, RCNVR) arrived at Bermuda from Halifax. (60)
28 Jul 1944 HMS Unruffled (Lt. R.F. Park, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Bermuda. (62)
